title: The Creation
description: God builds the entire universe in 6 days and takes a day off. The origin story of literally everything.

title: Adam & Eve
description: One rule. They had one rule. And they couldn't even do that. The original trust issues.

title: Cain & Abel
description: The first brothers. The first jealousy. The first murder. Things escalated terrifyingly fast.

title: Noah's Ark
description: God hits reset on the whole planet. One man builds a floating zoo and survives the apocalypse.

title: Tower of Babel
description: Humanity bands together to reach heaven. God scrambles their languages and scatters them everywhere.

title: Abraham's Call
description: A 75-year-old hears God say 'leave everything and go.' No map. No GPS. Just faith.

title: Abraham's Sister Lie
description: Abraham tells the Pharaoh his wife is his sister to save his own skin. Not his finest moment.

title: The Same Lie, Different King
description: Abraham pulls the exact same 'she's my sister' stunt with another king. You'd think he'd learn.

title: God's Promise of a Son
description: God promises a 100-year-old couple they'll have a baby. Sarah literally laughs in God's face.

title: Tired of Waiting
description: Abraham and Sarah get impatient with God's promise and try to force it with Hagar. It backfires spectacularly.

title: Sodom & Gomorrah
description: Two cities so far gone that God rains fire from the sky. Abraham negotiates, but there aren't even 10 good people.

title: Lot's Escape Gone Wrong
description: They escape the burning city, his wife looks back and turns to salt, then things get even weirder in a cave.

title: The Birth of Isaac
description: The miracle baby finally arrives. Laughter fills the tent where doubt lived for decades.

title: Abraham & Isaac
description: God asks Abraham to sacrifice his only son. The most intense trust test in history.

title: Isaac & Rebekah
description: A servant, some camels, a well, and a prayer. The original long-distance relationship works out perfectly.

title: Jacob & Esau
description: Twin brothers, a stolen blessing, and decades of beef. Family drama at its absolute finest.

title: Jacob's Ladder
description: On the run and sleeping on a rock, Jacob dreams of a stairway to heaven and God makes him a promise.

title: Jacob, Rachel & Leah
description: He works 7 years for the girl he loves, gets tricked into marrying her sister, then works 7 more. Love triangle from hell.

title: Jacob Wrestles God
description: An all-night wrestling match with a mysterious stranger. Jacob won't let go until he gets a blessing — and a new name.

title: Joseph & His Brothers
description: His brothers sold him into slavery because they were jealous of his outfit. Yes, a coat started all this.

title: Joseph in Egypt
description: From prison to running the entire country. The glow-up to end all glow-ups.

title: Joseph Forgives His Brothers
description: His brothers show up begging for food, not knowing he's basically the vice president now. The reveal is everything.

title: Jacob's Family Moves to Egypt
description: The whole clan relocates to Egypt. What starts as refuge becomes a 400-year stay nobody planned for.

title: Baby Moses
description: His mom hid him in a basket on the river to save his life. Pharaoh's daughter found him and raised him as royalty.

title: The Burning Bush
description: Moses sees a bush on fire that won't burn down. Then God starts talking from it. Career change incoming.

title: The 10 Plagues
description: God sent 10 increasingly unhinged plagues on Egypt. Blood rivers, frog invasions, total darkness. Pharaoh still said no.

title: Crossing the Red Sea
description: Trapped between the sea and an army. Moses raises his staff and the sea splits in two. The army doesn't make it.

title: Bread from Heaven, Water from Rock
description: No food? God sends bread from the sky every morning. No water? Moses hits a rock and it pours out.

title: The 10 Commandments
description: Thunder, fire, and God's voice from a mountain. Moses comes down with the ultimate terms of service carved in stone.

title: The Golden Calf
description: Moses was gone 40 days and the people immediately melted their jewelry into a fake god. The audacity was unreal.

title: God Gives a Second Chance
description: After the golden calf disaster, Moses goes back up the mountain. God renews the covenant. His face literally glows.

title: Building God's Tent
description: God gives blueprints for a portable temple. Every detail matters — this is where heaven meets earth in the desert.

title: The First Priests
description: Aaron and his sons get ordained. Holy fire falls. Then two of them do their own thing and it costs them everything.

title: Offerings & Sacrifices
description: Burnt offerings, grain offerings, peace offerings — God creates a whole language for getting close to him.

title: Clean & Unclean
description: Rules about food, skin, mold, and bodily fluids. Sounds weird, but it's God teaching them that holiness touches everything.

title: The Day of Atonement
description: One day a year, one priest enters God's presence to cover the sins of the entire nation. The weight of it is staggering.

title: Real-World Holiness
description: 'Love your neighbor as yourself' shows up here first. God spells out what justice and kindness look like in everyday life.

title: Sacred Feasts & Rhythms
description: Passover, harvest festivals, and the Day of Atonement. God builds a calendar around remembering what he's done.

title: Sabbath, Jubilee & Justice
description: Every 7 years, debts cancelled. Every 50 years, everything resets. God's economic policy is wild.

title: Organizing the Camp
description: Two million people in the desert need structure. God arranges every tribe around his presence at the center.

title: Camp Rules & the Cloud
description: Purity laws, trumpets, and a cloud that tells them when to move and when to stay. God leads in real-time.

title: Complaining, Craving & Chaos
description: They're sick of manna and want meat. God sends quail — so much quail it makes them sick. Be careful what you ask for.

title: 12 Spies & a 40-Year Detour
description: They send 12 spies into the promised land. 10 come back terrified, 2 say 'let's go.' Fear wins, and it costs them 40 years.

title: Rebellion & the Earth Opens Up
description: Korah challenges Moses' authority. The ground literally opens and swallows the rebels. God does not play.

title: Snakes, Water & the Long Road
description: More complaining, poisonous snakes, a bronze serpent on a pole, and Moses loses his cool at a rock.

title: A New Census, a New Start
description: The old generation is gone. A new census counts a new people ready to finally enter the promised land.

title: Make Your Promises Count
description: Laws about vows and offerings. God takes your word seriously — don't make promises you won't keep.

title: A Talking Donkey & a Hired Prophet
description: A prophet gets hired to curse Israel, but his donkey sees an angel and starts talking. You can't make this up.

title: When Curses Won't Stick
description: Balaam tries to curse Israel three times. Every time, blessings come out instead. The enemy switches to a dirtier tactic.

title: The Battle Against Midian
description: Justice is served on Midian for leading Israel astray. War in the ancient world was brutal and decisive.

title: Don't Get Comfortable Too Early
description: Two tribes want to settle east of the Jordan. Moses almost loses it — you can't quit before you cross the finish line.

title: Wandering the Desert
description: 40 years of walking because they couldn't trust God. The world's longest road trip with the worst vibes.

title: What Kind of Leaders God Wants
description: Kings, priests, prophets — God lays out the job description. Power must be leashed by Scripture and humility.

title: Rules for War
description: Even in battle, there are limits. Protect the vulnerable, spare the fruit trees, offer peace first.

title: Law for Messy Lives
description: Murder, marriage, lost property, stubborn kids — God has something to say about all of it. Holiness gets practical.

title: Community Standards
description: Honesty in court, limits on punishment, care for workers. Covenant love looks like a community that protects its weakest.

title: One God, One Place of Worship
description: No shrines on every hill. One name, one altar, one God. Identity starts with who you worship and how.

title: What God Really Wants
description: Not just rituals — heart-level loyalty. Love him, walk in his ways, serve with everything you've got.

title: Don't Let Success Ruin You
description: When the blessings roll in, don't forget who gave them. Gratitude is the guardrail against arrogance.

title: Remember Where You Started
description: You were slaves. You were nobody. Memory is the best armor against pride and the shortest path to gratitude.

title: Cancel Debts, Set People Free
description: Every 7 years, wipe the slate clean. Generosity isn't optional — it's how the community stays whole.

title: The Ultimate Choice
description: Blessing or curse, life or death. God puts both options on the table and says choose.

title: Cities of Refuge
description: Safe havens for accidental killers. Even ancient justice had nuance — intent matters.

title: Setting Boundaries
description: Property lines and power plays. God cares about borders, honesty, and keeping your word.

title: Fair Play & Fair Trade
description: Honest scales, fair treatment, no cheating in business or worship. Integrity in the small stuff matters.

title: Moses' Farewell Address
description: A song, a blessing over each tribe, one last look from a mountain. The final words of the greatest prophet.

title: Choose Life or Death
description: The choice is near you — in your mouth and in your heart. God begs them to choose life.

title: Blessings & Curses
description: Obey and flourish, disobey and everything falls apart. Moses lays out the two paths with brutal clarity.

title: The Warning Song
description: Moses writes a song predicting exactly how Israel will mess up. It's a warning disguised as poetry.

title: Moses' Final Days
description: He led them all the way there but never got to enter. He sees the promised land from a mountain and dies.

title: Joshua Takes Command
description: New leader, same God. Joshua steps up, sends spies, and a prostitute named Rahab hides them on her roof.

title: Crossing the Jordan
description: The priests step into the flooding river and it stops. They walk across on dry ground and stack stones to remember.

title: Joshua & Jericho
description: Walk around the city 7 days, blow trumpets, shout. The walls collapse. Military strategy: trust God.

title: Achan's Hidden Sin
description: One man steals forbidden loot and hides it under his tent. Israel loses the next battle because of it.

title: The Gibeonite Trick & the Sun Stands Still
description: A fake peace treaty fools Joshua. Then God stops the sun mid-sky so Israel can win a battle. Wild day.

title: Conquering the Land
description: Campaign after campaign, the map fills in. The promise takes shape one battle at a time.

title: Caleb Claims His Mountain
description: 85 years old, still swinging. Caleb says 'give me the hill with the giants on it' and takes it.

title: Claim Your Inheritance
description: The land is divided but some tribes are lazy about claiming it. Don't settle for tents when God promised territory.

title: Safe Cities, Sacred Spaces
description: Cities of refuge get assigned. Levite towns are scattered across the land. Justice and worship everywhere.

title: The Altar That Almost Started a War
description: Two and a half tribes build an altar and the rest of Israel almost goes to civil war over it. Talking it out saves them.

title: Joshua's Final Challenge
description: 'Choose this day who you will serve.' Joshua draws a line and dares Israel to commit.

title: The Cycle of Disobedience
description: Sin, suffering, crying out, rescue, repeat. Israel falls into a pattern that defines an entire era.

title: Deborah & the Tent Peg
description: A female judge leads Israel to battle. Then another woman ends the war with a tent stake. Don't underestimate them.

title: Gideon vs. the Midianites
description: God cuts Gideon's army from 32,000 to 300. Torches, jars, and trumpets vs a massive army. God loves impossible odds.

title: When Victory Goes to Your Head
description: Gideon wins but makes an idol from the gold. Victory without humility turns sour fast.

title: The Bramble King
description: Abimelech murders 70 brothers to become king. A parable about a bramble bush predicts his downfall. Ego destroys.

title: Jephthah's Reckless Vow
description: A rough-edged warrior makes a vow he can't take back. It costs him the thing he loves most.

title: Samson & Delilah
description: The strongest man alive had one weakness — a woman who asked his secret three times and he still told her.

title: Everyone Did What They Wanted
description: No king, no rules, pure chaos. Israel hits rock bottom. The horror shows what happens without God.

title: Homemade Religion
description: A guy builds his own shrine, hires his own priest, and a whole tribe steals it. DIY worship leads nowhere good.

title: Ruth & Naomi
description: A foreign widow chooses loyalty over comfort. 'Where you go, I go.' Her faithfulness changes everything.

title: David & Goliath
description: A teenager with a slingshot vs a 9-foot giant in full armor. Everyone thought he was crazy. He wasn't.

title: Saul's Last Stand
description: The first king falls in battle. David mourns his enemy with one of the most beautiful laments ever written.

title: Two Kings, Two Armies
description: Israel splits between David and Saul's son. Rival generals, risky diplomacy, and a long power struggle.

title: David Becomes King
description: The shepherd boy finally takes the throne. Israel unites under one crown after years of civil war.

title: David Dances Before the Ark
description: The Ark comes to Jerusalem. David dances with zero shame. His wife watches from the window and judges him hard.

title: God's Promise to David
description: David wants to build God a house. God flips it — 'I'll build YOUR house. Your throne will last forever.'

title: Kindness to Mephibosheth
description: At the peak of his power, David hunts down Saul's disabled grandson — not to kill him, but to invite him to dinner forever.

title: David & Bathsheba
description: Even the greatest king fell hard. Power, lust, a cover-up, and a murder. Then a prophet walks in and calls it out.

title: Absalom's Rise
description: David's family fractures. Rape, revenge, exile — the consequences of Bathsheba ripple through his own house.

title: Absalom's Rebellion
description: David's own son steals the hearts of the people and forces the king to flee his own capital. Charisma without character.

title: A Father's Worst Battle
description: David's army fights Absalom's. David wins the war but loses his son. 'O Absalom, my son, my son.' Devastating.

title: David's Broken Return
description: The king comes back to Jerusalem, but nothing is the same. Forgiveness is messy and repair takes longer than ruin.

title: David's Last Battles
description: Giants fall, debts are settled, and psalms of thanks close out an era of warfare.

title: David's Final Mistake
description: David orders a census out of pride. A plague follows. He buys a threshing floor that becomes the future temple site.

title: Solomon's Wisdom
description: God says 'ask for anything.' Solomon picks wisdom. Then immediately proves it with the wildest judgment call ever.

title: Elijah on Mount Carmel
description: One prophet vs 450 prophets of Baal. Build altars, call for fire. Only one God answers. Elijah doesn't flinch.

title: The Kingdom Splits
description: Solomon's son ignores wise advice and doubles down on harsh rule. One nation becomes two over one dumb decision.

title: Kings Who Copied the Worst
description: One bad king after another copies the sins of those before. Cosmetic religion can't cure deep rot.

title: Decay, Coups & Leprosy
description: Internal corruption invites external disaster. The monarchy slowly rots from the inside out.

title: Manasseh Hits Reverse
description: A son undoes his father's reforms and drags the nation to its worst spiritual low. One generation can undo decades of progress.

title: Josiah Finds the Lost Book
description: Workers find the Book of the Law buried in temple rubble. When the king hears it read, he tears his clothes and reforms everything.

title: Josiah's Last Battle
description: The revival king wages one final war. Altars are smashed, hearts are called home, but the clock is ticking on Judah.

title: Hezekiah's Extra Years
description: God gives Hezekiah 15 more years, then he blows it by showing off his treasures to the wrong people.

title: One Angel, One Army Destroyed
description: 185,000 Assyrian soldiers surround Jerusalem. Hezekiah prays. One angel. One night. The entire army is gone by morning.

title: Hezekiah vs. the Empire
description: A faithful king stares down the Assyrian war machine. Intimidation letters arrive. He spreads them before God and prays.

title: The Northern Kingdom Falls
description: Centuries of bad kings catch up. Assyria conquers Israel and scatters the 10 tribes. They never come back.

title: Jerusalem Burns
description: Babylon breaks through the walls and burns the temple to the ground. The people are dragged into exile. Everything is gone.

title: Daniel & the Lions' Den
description: They threw him in a pit of lions because he wouldn't stop praying. The lions didn't touch him.

title: Esther Saves Her People
description: A secret Jewish queen risks everything to stop a genocide. She walks in uninvited to the king. Ultimate main character energy.

title: Jonah & the Whale
description: God said go left. Jonah went right. A giant fish swallowed him for 3 days. Lesson learned the hard way.

title: An Angel Visits Zechariah
description: An old priest gets an angel visit promising a miracle baby. He doubts it and loses his voice for 9 months.

title: An Angel Visits Mary
description: A teenage girl gets told she'll carry God's son. She says yes. Joseph almost walks away. The most important pregnancy in history.

title: The Birth of John the Baptist
description: The miracle baby arrives. Zechariah gets his voice back and prophesies. The forerunner of Jesus is born.

title: The Birth of Jesus
description: A teenage girl, no room at the inn, a baby born in a barn with animals. Angels sing to shepherds. Christmas but make it real.

title: Simeon & Anna at the Temple
description: Two elderly believers waited their whole lives to see the Messiah. They recognize a baby in his parents' arms.

title: The Wise Men
description: Star-following scholars from the east bring gold, frankincense, and myrrh. King Herod is not happy about the competition.

title: Flight to Egypt
description: An angel warns Joseph in a dream. The family flees to Egypt in the night. Herod slaughters every baby boy in Bethlehem.

title: Boy Jesus at the Temple
description: Twelve-year-old Jesus stays behind in the temple, debating scholars. His parents panic. He says 'I'm in my Father's house.'

title: John the Baptist Preaches
description: A wild man in the desert wearing camel hair, eating locusts, screaming 'repent.' He's preparing the way for someone bigger.

title: John Declares 'The Lamb of God'
description: John sees Jesus coming and says 'Look — the Lamb of God.' A prophet points past himself to the one who matters.

title: Jesus Gets Baptized
description: Jesus walks into the Jordan River and the sky opens. A dove descends. God says 'that's my son' out loud.

title: 40 Days in the Desert
description: Starving and alone, Jesus faces the devil's three best tricks. He shuts them all down with Scripture.

title: Meeting the First Disciples
description: Simple invitations — 'come and see' — change the course of history. Fishermen drop their nets and follow.

title: Water to Wine
description: Jesus' first miracle happens at a wedding party because his mom basically peer-pressured him into it. Iconic.

title: Nicodemus Comes at Night
description: A religious leader sneaks to Jesus after dark. 'You must be born again.' The conversation that explains everything.

title: Jesus Flips Tables in the Temple
description: Jesus walks into the temple, sees it turned into a marketplace, and starts flipping tables. Nobody stops him.

title: John the Baptist's Last Words
description: As his own following shrinks, John says 'He must increase, I must decrease.' A prophet who knew his role.

title: Healing at the Pool of Bethesda
description: A man waits 38 years by a pool hoping for healing. Jesus walks up and says 'do you want to get well?' One word and it's done.

title: The Miraculous Catch of Fish
description: Professional fishermen catch nothing all night. Jesus says try again. The nets nearly break from the haul.

title: Casting Out an Evil Spirit
description: In the synagogue, a demon screams at Jesus. One command and it's gone. The crowd is stunned — who IS this guy?

title: Peter's Mother-in-Law Healed
description: Jesus touches her hand, the fever leaves, and she immediately gets up and starts serving dinner. Word spreads fast.

title: Touching the Leper
description: Nobody touches lepers. Jesus reaches out his hand. 'I am willing. Be clean.' The outcast is restored.

title: Through the Roof
description: Four friends can't get through the crowd, so they rip open the roof and lower their paralyzed friend to Jesus. That's friendship.

title: Matthew Leaves Everything
description: A hated tax collector hears 'follow me,' walks away from the money table, and throws Jesus a dinner party with all his shady friends.

title: The Sabbath Showdowns
description: Jesus heals on the Sabbath and the religious leaders lose their minds. Rules were made for people, not the other way around.

title: Picking the Twelve
description: After a night of prayer, Jesus picks his squad — fishermen, a tax collector, a zealot, and a traitor. Interesting choices.

title: The Centurion's Faith
description: A Roman soldier says 'just say the word and my servant will be healed.' Jesus is blown away — this outsider has more faith than Israel.

title: The Widow's Son Lives Again
description: A funeral procession. A grieving mother. Jesus touches the coffin, says 'get up,' and the dead man sits up. The crowd freaks out.

title: Deaf Ears Opened
description: A deaf man with a speech impediment meets Jesus. Ears open, tongue loosens, and the crowd can't stop talking about it.

title: Jesus Stops a Storm
description: Waves crashing, boat sinking, disciples screaming. Jesus wakes up from a nap and tells the storm to shut up. It does.

title: Legion & the Pigs
description: A man possessed by thousands of demons lives in a graveyard. Jesus frees him in one sentence. The demons go into pigs.

title: Jairus's Daughter & the Bleeding Woman
description: One desperate father and one daring woman. Both need a miracle. Both get one. Faith interrupts death.

title: Healing the Official's Son
description: A royal official begs Jesus to heal his dying kid. Jesus says 'go home, your son is fine.' He is.

title: Demons & Accusations
description: Jesus casts out a demon and the Pharisees accuse him of working for Satan. Jesus dismantles their logic in seconds.

title: John the Baptist Beheaded
description: A birthday party, a dance, and a horrifying request. The greatest prophet loses his head because of a grudge.

title: Jesus Feeds 5,000
description: 5 loaves, 2 fish, 5,000 hungry people. He made it work. And there were leftovers.

title: Jesus Walks on Water
description: Middle of a storm. Disciples panicking. Then they see someone walking on the waves toward them.

title: Jesus Feeds 4,000
description: Different crowd, same compassion. A few loaves, a few fish, thousands fed. Bread becomes a recurring theme.

title: Bread of Life
description: After the miracle meal, Jesus says 'I AM the bread of life.' The crowd wanted food. He offered himself. Many walk away.

title: The Purity Debate
description: The Pharisees obsess over hand-washing rituals. Jesus says it's not what goes into you that defiles — it's what comes out of your heart.

title: A Mother's Desperate Faith
description: A Syrophoenician woman begs Jesus to heal her daughter. He tests her. She fires back with one of the best comebacks in the Bible.

title: Peter's Big Confession
description: 'Who do you say I am?' Peter nails it — 'You're the Christ.' Then Jesus drops the first prediction of his death.

title: The Transfiguration
description: Jesus takes three disciples up a mountain. His face blazes like the sun. Moses and Elijah appear. Peter wants to build tents.

title: The Boy with a Demon
description: The disciples can't heal a demon-possessed boy. Jesus does it instantly and says 'this kind requires prayer and fasting.'

title: Jesus Predicts His Death
description: Jesus tells the disciples exactly what's coming — betrayal, death, resurrection. They don't understand. He says it three times.

title: The Fish with a Coin
description: Tax collectors come asking. Jesus tells Peter to catch a fish — there'll be a coin in its mouth. There is. Bill paid.

title: The Festival Showdown
description: Jesus shows up at the Feast of Tabernacles and the crowds are split — prophet, deceiver, or Messiah? Tension is thick.

title: Born Blind, Now Sees
description: Jesus heals a man blind from birth. The Pharisees interrogate him relentlessly. His answer is simple: 'I was blind. Now I see.'

title: The Good Shepherd
description: 'I am the good shepherd. The good shepherd lays down his life for the sheep.' Jesus defines leadership by sacrifice.

title: Sending Out 72 Missionaries
description: Jesus sends 72 followers ahead of him in pairs. They come back amazed — even demons obey them.

title: The Good Samaritan
description: A man gets robbed and left for dead. Religious people walk past. The despised outsider stops to help. 'Who was the neighbor?'

title: Mary & Martha
description: One sister is hustling in the kitchen, the other is sitting at Jesus' feet. Martha complains. Jesus sides with Mary.

title: Teaching Them to Pray
description: 'Lord, teach us to pray.' Jesus gives them the simplest, most powerful prayer in history. Then tells a story about persistence.

title: Casting Out Demons & Taking Heat
description: Jesus drives out a demon. Some say he's using Satan's power. Jesus dismantles the accusation and warns about empty houses.

title: Calling Out the Pharisees
description: Jesus accepts a dinner invite then absolutely roasts the Pharisees to their faces. Hypocrisy gets exposed at the table.

title: The Woman at the Well
description: A Samaritan outcast meets Jesus at a well. He knows everything about her. She becomes the first evangelist.

title: Lazarus Lives Again
description: Dead four days, sealed in a tomb. Jesus weeps, then shouts 'Lazarus, come out!' And he does. The ultimate mic drop.

title: Go Ahead, Throw a Stone
description: A woman caught in adultery is dragged before Jesus. 'Let the one without sin throw first.' One by one, they walk away.

title: The Wedding Feast Parables
description: Invitations go out to the VIPs. They all make excuses. So the host invites everyone from the streets instead.

title: The Narrow Door
description: 'Strive to enter through the narrow door.' Many will try and fail. Urgency rings out — don't wait.

title: Counting the Cost
description: Following Jesus isn't casual. Count the cost first. He's not looking for fans — he wants people who'll carry a cross.

title: The Prodigal Son
description: He took his inheritance, blew it all, and came crawling back to eat pig food. His dad threw him a party anyway.

title: The Shrewd Manager
description: A manager about to get fired cuts deals with his boss's debtors. Jesus praises the hustle. Wait, what?

title: The Rich Man & Lazarus
description: A rich man ignores a beggar at his gate. They both die. The reversal on the other side is permanent.

title: Zacchaeus Climbs a Tree
description: A short, rich tax collector climbs a sycamore to see Jesus. Jesus spots him and invites himself to dinner. Everything changes.

title: The Cursed Fig Tree & Temple Fury
description: Jesus curses a fruitless fig tree and clears the temple of merchants. Both acts say the same thing: bear fruit or be cut off.

title: Palm Sunday
description: Jesus rides into Jerusalem on a donkey. Crowds wave palms and shout 'Hosanna!' The king arrives — but not how anyone expected.

title: Caesar's Coin
description: They try to trap Jesus with a political question. He asks for a coin. 'Give to Caesar what's Caesar's, and to God what's God's.' Mic drop.

title: Faith, Figs & Loaded Questions
description: Religious leaders fire trap after trap at Jesus. He answers every one and fires back harder.

title: Marriage & the Afterlife
description: The Sadducees think they've got Jesus with a brain teaser about marriage after death. He shuts it down in one sentence.

title: The Greatest Commandment
description: 'Love God with everything. Love your neighbor as yourself.' The entire law and prophets hang on these two lines.

title: Whose Son Is the Messiah?
description: Jesus asks the Pharisees a riddle about David's son. Nobody can answer. He reveals the Messiah is bigger than they imagined.

title: Signs of the End
description: On the Mount of Olives, Jesus describes the end of the world — wars, earthquakes, deception. 'Stay ready. You don't know the day.'

title: The Plot Against Jesus
description: The religious leaders have had enough. They start scheming in secret to arrest and kill Jesus before the festival ends.

title: Judas Makes a Deal
description: Thirty silver coins. That's the price Judas puts on Jesus. He walks to the priests and offers to hand him over.

title: Anointed at Bethany
description: A woman pours expensive perfume on Jesus. The disciples say it's wasteful. Jesus says she's preparing him for burial.

title: The Last Supper
description: His final meal with his 12 closest friends. Bread and wine become his body and blood. One of them is about to betray him.

title: Gethsemane
description: 'Take this cup from me. But not my will — yours.' In crushing sorrow, Jesus chooses obedience over escape.

title: Arrested in the Garden
description: A kiss from Judas. Torches and swords. Peter swings a sword and cuts off an ear. Jesus heals it and surrenders.

title: Before the Sanhedrin
description: False witnesses, loaded questions, and one honest answer: 'I am.' The high priest tears his robes. Guilty of blasphemy.

title: Peter's Three Denials
description: 'I don't know him.' Three times. Then the rooster crows. Peter remembers and breaks down crying.

title: Jesus Before Pilate
description: Pilate finds no fault in him. The crowd screams for crucifixion. He washes his hands and hands Jesus over.

title: The Crucifixion
description: They mocked him, beat him, and nailed him to a cross. The sky went dark. He said 'It is finished' and gave up his spirit.

title: Buried in a Borrowed Tomb
description: Joseph of Arimathea takes the body down, wraps it in linen, and places it in his own new tomb. A stone seals the entrance.

title: The Resurrection
description: Three days later, the tomb is empty. He's alive. Everything he said was true. The greatest comeback in history.

title: The Road to Emmaus
description: Two heartbroken disciples walk home. A stranger joins them and explains the Scriptures. When he breaks bread, they realize — it's Jesus.

title: Doubting Thomas
description: 'Unless I put my finger in the nail holes, I won't believe.' Jesus shows up and says 'go ahead.' Thomas falls to his knees.

title: The Great Commission
description: 'Go and make disciples of all nations.' Jesus gives the final mission statement before leaving earth.

title: The Ascension
description: Jesus leads his disciples to a hillside, blesses them, and rises into the sky. Two angels say 'he'll be back.'

title: Pentecost
description: The Holy Spirit arrives like fire and wind. Everyone starts speaking languages they never learned. The church is born.

title: The Beautiful Gate Miracle
description: A disabled beggar asks Peter for money. Peter says 'I don't have money, but I have something better.' The man walks.

title: Ananias & Sapphira
description: A couple lies about their donation to the church. They both drop dead. God is not messing around with fakes in the early church.

title: Stephen, the First Martyr
description: A man full of faith and fire preaches the truth. They stone him to death. He forgives them while dying.

title: The Church Scatters & Philip
description: Persecution scatters believers everywhere. Philip ends up baptizing an Ethiopian official on a desert road. The gospel spreads.

title: Saul Meets Jesus on the Road
description: A Christian-hunter gets blinded by a light. Jesus says 'why are you persecuting me?' Saul becomes Paul. The greatest 180 in history.

title: Peter's Miracles in Lydda & Joppa
description: Peter heals a paralyzed man and raises a dead woman. The apostles are walking in the same power Jesus had.

title: Cornelius & the Sheet Vision
description: God sends Peter a vision of forbidden food and says 'eat.' The message: salvation isn't just for Jews anymore.

title: The Antioch Church
description: A diverse church launches in Antioch. For the first time, followers of Jesus are called 'Christians.'

title: James Killed, Peter Escapes
description: King Herod executes James. He arrests Peter too — but an angel breaks him out of prison in the middle of the night.

title: Paul's First Missionary Journey
description: Paul and Barnabas hit the road. New churches spring up, old habits resist, and the gospel goes where it's never been.

title: The Jerusalem Council
description: The early church debates the biggest question: do non-Jews have to follow Jewish law? They land on grace.

title: Earthquake at Philippi
description: Paul and Silas are in prison, singing hymns at midnight. An earthquake blows the doors open. The jailer converts.

title: Thessalonica Riot & Berean Scholars
description: One city riots, the next one fact-checks Scripture daily. Two very different receptions for the same message.

title: Athens: The Unknown God
description: Paul finds an altar labeled 'To an Unknown God.' He says 'let me introduce him.' Philosophy meets revelation.

title: Riot in Ephesus & the Bonfire
description: Believers burn their magic books worth millions. Silversmiths riot because they're losing money. Power confronts profit.

title: Eutychus Falls Out a Window
description: Paul preaches so long that a young man falls asleep, falls out a third-story window, and dies. Paul raises him back.

title: Farewell at Miletus
description: Paul says goodbye to the church leaders at Miletus. There are tears on the shore. He knows he won't see them again.

title: Arrested at the Temple
description: Paul visits the temple in Jerusalem and a mob drags him out. Roman soldiers save his life. The trials begin.

title: Trials Before Governors
description: Paul gets passed from one Roman official to another. Each one finds no fault, but nobody sets him free. He appeals to Caesar.

title: Shipwreck & Malta
description: A brutal storm, 14 days lost at sea, the ship breaks apart. Everyone makes it to shore. A snake bites Paul. He shakes it off.

title: Paul Under House Arrest
description: Chained to a Roman guard in a rented room, Paul keeps preaching. Chains can't stop the word.

title: James Writes to the Church
description: Practical wisdom for scattered believers. Faith without action is dead. Control your tongue. Help the poor. No excuses.

title: Peter's Final Letter
description: An aging apostle warns about false teachers and reminds the church: he SAW Jesus transfigured on that mountain. This isn't fiction.

title: Paul's Last Letter
description: From a Roman prison, Paul writes to Timothy one final time. 'I have fought the good fight. I have finished the race.'

title: John's Exile & Revelation
description: Exiled to an island, the last living apostle receives a vision of heaven, judgment, and the end of all things.

title: The New Heaven & New Earth
description: God wipes every tear. No more death, pain, or crying. Everything is made new. The story ends where it began — God with his people.
